title: Insertion sort is correct: output is sorted and a permutation of the input
statement_informal: Insertion sort produces a sorted list that is a permutation of the input (no element lost, duplicated, or invented). Correctness companion to insertion-sort-comparisons (the n(n-1)/2 cost). isort_correct: Sorted (isort l) /\ Permutation (isort l) l.

cost_model.notes: isort (x::t) = insert x (isort t). insert_sorted (case-split on the head comparison) gives sortedness; insert_perm (perm_skip + perm_swap) gives the permutation.

references: Insertion sort (TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.2.1). Full functional correctness (sorted + permutation); the comparison count is work-units/insertion-sort-comparisons.
